direction.label: Luxury Brand
direction.summary: Premium buyers decide fast — optimize for trust first.

decisions.title: Homepage feels more premium
decisions.detail: Hero rewritten, typography refined, spacing tightened.
decisions.why: Premium buyers usually decide in under 10 seconds — lead with trust.

decisions.title: Reviews moved higher
decisions.why: Reviews are often the strongest trust signal.

decisions.title: Booking asks fewer questions
decisions.detail: Simplified flow; fewer fields before confirmation.

decisions.title: Portfolio starts with luxury work
decisions.detail: Hero images reordered toward elevated jobs.
decisions.why: Visual proof should match the positioning.

decisions.title: Package names updated
decisions.detail: Clearer tier language; pricing presentation strengthened.
decisions.why: Premium packaging is positioning, not just a price list.

decisions.title: Button style modernized
decisions.why: One clear next step reduces hesitation.

decisions.title: Owner asked for a more premium website feel.
decisions.why: Owner asked for a more premium website feel.

decisions.title: Premium feel usually starts with hero headline tone.
decisions.why: Premium feel usually starts with hero headline tone.

score.note: Business quality — not SEO, not Lighthouse.

conversation.message: I don't like the new typography.
Got it — I updated typography and remembered that preference for next builds.
Keep the luxury layout but use brighter photography.
Combined — luxury layout with brighter photography. Preferences saved.
Remove reviews.

I can do that.

I don't recommend it because Reviews are your strongest trust signal — removing them usually hurts conversion.

conversation.message:
Here's what I changed across your business — not just the website.

✓ Homepage feels more premium
✓ Reviews moved higher
✓ Booking asks fewer questions
✓ Portfolio starts with luxury work
✓ Package names updated
✓ Button style modernized
✓ Owner asked for a more premium website feel.
✓ Premium feel usually starts with hero headline tone.

Why?
Premium buyers decide fast — optimize for trust first.

What do you think?
Make it more friendly.

I think that means warmer language and softer proof — without dropping the craft. I updated the Creative Direction toward Friendly Premium.

What do you think?
Actually keep it premium.
I'll combine both — friendly where it helps, premium where it converts. Preview updated.
